title: Sandwich Codes
description: An AI project portfolio and editorial website with a Rugrats-inspired postmodern aesthetic — handmade, asymmetrical, tactile, and expressive.

body.raw:
## Overview Sandwich Codes is an AI project portfolio and editorial website that doubles as its own showcase. It's the site you're looking at right now — a surreal 90s cartoon art department transformed into an experimental AI design studio. The aesthetic is deliberately off-kilter: Rugrats-era Nickelodeon meets editorial magazine meets sketchbook. Cards rotate slightly. Typography is oversized and asymmetrical. Doodles and scribbles float in the margins. Every surface has a paper-grain texture. The goal is to feel personal, strange, intelligent, and handmade — the opposite of a generic SaaS landing page. Underneath the visual chaos is a serious technical foundation. Content is authored in MDX and processed by Contentlayer at build time. Pages are statically generated with Next.js App Router. Animations use Framer Motion spring physics. The design system is built on Tailwind CSS with a custom token layer for colours, typography, shadows, and decorative components. ![Sandwich Codes homepage with hero section, featured projects, and hand-drawn decorative accents](/images/projects/sandwich-dot-codes-homepage.png) ## The Problem It Solves Most AI portfolio sites fall into one of two traps: either they're sterile, corporate-feeling dashboards with clean lines and muted palettes, or they're flashy-but-unreadable experiments that sacrifice usability for visual spectacle. Sandwich Codes occupies the middle ground — expressive and visually loud, but still readable, navigable, and fast. The site exists to showcase other projects (Expecto, Splinched, Sound of Wall) and to serve as a technical artifact in its own right. Every design decision — from the crooked card rotations to the hand-drawn SVG doodles — is implemented as a reusable component, not a one-off hack. ## Design System The design philosophy can be summarised as "intentional imperfection." Key principles: - **Asymmetry over symmetry** — Layouts deliberately avoid rigid centring. Elements rotate, offset, and overlap. - **Tactile surfaces** — Paper-grain textures, thick borders, layered shadows, and tape-strip effects make everything feel physical. - **Expressive typography** — Fraunces (a variable optical-size serif) handles headings with oversized, tightly-tracked weight. Inter handles body text. Line heights and spacing vary to create editorial rhythm. - **Dusty pastels** — The palette avoids pure white, pure black, and neon. Colours are warm and muted: mustard (`#D6B347`), teal (`#6F9D9A`), salmon (`#D98B73`), slime green (`#9FB06F`), lavender (`#B8A7CC`), and sky blue (`#9BB7D4`) sit on a cream background (`#F5EFE4`). - **Hand-drawn accents** — SVG doodles (stars, squiggles, circles, arrows, dot clusters) are scattered throughout as decorative elements at various scales, from 32px inline accents to 420px background watermarks. ### Decorative Component Library The site ships with a dedicated `/components/decorative` module containing reusable visual primitives: - **StickerTag** — Coloured label badges with slight rotation, used for tech stacks and status indicators. - **DoodleAccent** — Hand-drawn SVG shapes (star, circle, squiggle, arrow, x, dot-cluster) at configurable sizes and colours. - **TapeFrame** — A container that renders translucent tape strips at its corners, simulating photos taped to a surface. - **WobbleCard** — An interactive card that straightens and scales on hover with spring physics. - **PaperCard** — A static card variant with paper texture, thick borders, and tactile shadows. - **CrookedDivider** — Section dividers in scribble, wavy, and zigzag variants. - **NoiseOverlay** — A full-page subtle noise texture layer. - **ScribbleArrow** — A hand-drawn directional arrow. ## Content Architecture All content lives in the repository as MDX files, processed by Contentlayer at build time. Three content types are defined: - **Projects** (`/content/projects/`) — Full case studies with frontmatter for title, description, date, tags, status, cover image, tech stack, GitHub URL, and optional live demo link. - **Articles** (`/content/writing/`) — Long-form writing with reading time computed automatically. - **Experiments** (`/content/experiments/`) — Smaller playground projects and prototypes. Each content type has a corresponding schema in `contentlayer.config.ts` with required fields, computed fields (slug, reading time, URL), and MDX plugins for syntax highlighting, GitHub-flavoured markdown, and auto-linked headings. ## Technical Architecture ### Rendering Strategy The site uses Next.js App Router with a hybrid rendering approach: - **Static generation** for all content pages (projects, articles, experiments). Contentlayer generates typed objects at build time, and `generateStaticParams()` creates routes for each slug. - **Server components** by default. Client components are isolated to interactive elements: animations (`framer-motion`), search, theme toggle, and GitHub timeline polling. - **API routes** for GitHub data fetching (`/api/github-timeline`, `/api/github-languages`) with client-side polling every 90 seconds. ### GitHub Integration The homepage includes a live GitHub activity section that fetches public events (commits, pull requests, releases) from the GitHub REST API. A companion language chart aggregates bytes-per-language across all non-fork repositories. Data refreshes server-side every 60 seconds and client-side every 90 seconds. ### Styling Tailwind CSS handles all styling through a custom configuration that extends the default theme with: - Custom colour tokens mapped to the dusty-pastel palette. - `Fraunces` and `Inter` loaded via `next/font` with CSS variable injection. - Custom box-shadow utilities (`tactile`, `tactile-lg`, `tactile-sm`, `paper`) for the layered shadow effect. - A `.paper-grain` utility class and `.noise-overlay` for texture. - `.sticker`, `.tape-strip`, and `.crooked-border` component classes in the global CSS base layer. - Dark mode support via the `.dark` class with inverted backgrounds and preserved accent colours. ### Animation Framer Motion powers all motion on the site. The motion system follows strict rules: playful, springy, and imperfect. Allowed patterns include fade-up reveals with staggered delays, hover wobble effects, soft scaling, and gentle parallax. Corporate-smooth easing and sci-fi effects are explicitly avoided. ## Style Guide This section serves as the authoritative reference for code conventions across the Sandwich Codes codebase. Every rule here reflects what the code actually does, not aspirational goals. ### Formatting Enforced by Prettier (`.prettierrc`): | Rule | Value | |---|---| | Semicolons | Always | | Quotes | Double quotes | | Trailing commas | Always (objects, arrays, params) | | Tab width | 2 spaces | | Print width | 100 characters | | Line endings | LF | Run `npm run format` before committing. No ESLint config exists — rely on `next lint` defaults and Prettier for enforcement. ### Naming Conventions | Entity | Convention | Example | |---|---|---| | Component files | PascalCase | `ProjectCard.tsx`, `WobbleCard.tsx` | | Utility files | camelCase | `utils.ts`, `config.ts`, `github.ts` | | Component functions | PascalCase named export | `export function ProjectCard()` | | Props interfaces | PascalCase + `Props` suffix | `interface ProjectCardProps` | | Type definitions | PascalCase | `Project`, `NavItem`, `SiteConfig` | | Constants / config objects | camelCase | `siteConfig`, `statusColors` | | Variant maps | camelCase + `Styles` or `Colors` suffix | `variantStyles`, `statusColors` | | CSS custom classes | kebab-case | `.paper-grain`, `.noise-overlay`, `.tape-strip` | | CSS variables | kebab-case, double-dash prefix | `--font-fraunces`, `--font-inter` | | MDX content files | kebab-case | `sandwich-dot-codes.mdx`, `expecto.mdx` | | Route directories | kebab-case | `src/app/projects/[slug]/page.tsx` | ### Component Architecture #### Named Exports Only Every component uses a named function export. Default exports are not used anywhere in the codebase. ```tsx // Correct export function ProjectCard({ project, index = 0 }: ProjectCardProps) { return
...
; } // Incorrect — never do this export default function ProjectCard() { ... } ``` #### Props Interface Pattern Props are defined as an `interface` directly above the component. Components extend `React.HTMLAttributes` when they need to accept standard HTML attributes. ```tsx interface StickerTagProps extends React.HTMLAttributes { variant?: StickerVariant; rotation?: number; } export function StickerTag({ variant = "default", rotation = -2, className, children, ...props }: StickerTagProps) { // ... } ``` #### Variant Pattern Components that have visual variants use string literal union types and a `Record` map for styles: ```tsx type StickerVariant = "default" | "salmon" | "teal" | "mustard" | "slime" | "lavender" | "muted"; const variantStyles: Record = { default: "bg-background text-foreground", salmon: "bg-salmon text-foreground", // ... }; ``` #### Polymorphic `as` Prop Layout primitives accept an `as` prop for rendering as a different HTML element: ```tsx interface ContainerProps extends React.HTMLAttributes { as?: React.ElementType; size?: "default" | "narrow" | "wide"; } export function Container({ as: Component = "div", ... }: ContainerProps) { return {children}; } ``` ### "use client" Directive Only components that require client-side interactivity include the `"use client"` directive. This includes: - Components using `useState`, `useEffect`, or other React hooks - Components using Framer Motion (`motion`, `AnimatePresence`) - Components using browser APIs (`window`, `document`) - Components using Next.js client hooks (`useRouter`, `useSearchParams`, `usePathname`) Page components and data-fetching components remain as server components by default. Client interactivity is always delegated to the smallest possible leaf component. ### Class Name Merging All conditional class merging uses the `cn()` utility from `@/lib/utils`, which combines `clsx` and `tailwind-merge`: ```tsx import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";
``` Never use raw string concatenation or template literals for class composition. ### Imports Import order (enforced by convention, not a plugin): 1. React / Next.js imports 2. Third-party libraries (`framer-motion`, `lucide-react`, `date-fns`) 3. Contentlayer generated types (`contentlayer/generated`) 4. Internal components (`@/components/...`) 5. Internal utilities (`@/lib/...`) 6. Types / constants ```tsx import { useState } from "react"; import Link from "next/link"; import Image from "next/image"; import { motion } from "framer-motion"; import { ExternalLink, Github } from "lucide-react"; import type { Project } from "contentlayer/generated"; import { StickerTag } from "@/components/decorative/StickerTag"; import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"; ``` Always use the `@/` path alias. Never use relative paths like `../../lib/utils`. ### TypeScript - **Strict mode** is enabled. No `any` types. - **Contentlayer types** are imported with `import type` from `contentlayer/generated`. - **Custom types** live in `src/types/index.ts` and are exported as named types. - **Enum-like values** use string literal union types, not TypeScript `enum`: ```tsx status: "active" | "experimental" | "archived" ``` - **Optional fields** use `?` in the interface, never `| undefined`. ### Styling #### Tailwind-First All styling is done with Tailwind utility classes. Custom CSS is only used for effects that Tailwind cannot express (SVG textures, pseudo-element overlays, scroll styling). #### Class Order Follow this implicit ordering when writing Tailwind classes: 1. Layout (`flex`, `grid`, `block`, `relative`, `absolute`) 2. Sizing (`w-`, `h-`, `min-`, `max-`) 3. Spacing (`p-`, `m-`, `gap-`) 4. Typography (`font-`, `text-`, `leading-`, `tracking-`) 5. Visual (`bg-`, `border-`, `shadow-`, `opacity-`) 6. States (`hover:`, `focus:`, `dark:`) 7. Transitions (`transition-`, `duration-`) #### Design Tokens Always use the custom design tokens, never raw hex values in components: | Token | Tailwind Class | Hex | |---|---|---| | Background | `bg-background` / `bg-background-dark` | `#F5EFE4` / `#1a1816` | | Text | `text-foreground` / `text-foreground-dark` | `#292524` / `#e8e0d4` | | Accent | `text-mustard`, `bg-mustard` | `#D6B347` | | Link | `text-teal` | `#6F9D9A` | | Experiment | `text-salmon`, `bg-salmon` | `#D98B73` | | Active | `text-slime`, `bg-slime` | `#9FB06F` | | Decorative | `text-lavender`, `bg-lavender` | `#B8A7CC` | | Secondary text | `text-muted` | `#7C736C` | | Border | `border-border` | `#463F3A` | #### Border Width The site uses `border-3` (3px) as the standard border width, not the default `border` (1px). This is defined as a custom Tailwind utility. #### Box Shadows Four custom shadow utilities are available: | Utility | Usage | |---|---| | `shadow-tactile` | Standard card shadow (4px offset) | | `shadow-tactile-lg` | Hover/enhanced state (6px offset) | | `shadow-tactile-sm` | Small elements, tags (2px offset) | | `shadow-paper` | Subtle layered paper effect | #### Typography Scale Three custom fluid font sizes using `clamp()`: | Utility | Size Range | Usage | |---|---|---| | `text-display` | 3rem — 7rem | Hero headlines only | | `text-headline` | 2rem — 4.5rem | Section headings | | `text-subhead` | 1.5rem — 2.5rem | Subsection headings | Body text uses Tailwind defaults (`text-base`, `text-sm`, `text-lg`). #### Font Families | Utility | Font | Usage | |---|---|---| | `font-heading` | Fraunces (variable serif) | All headings, labels, tags | | `font-body` | Inter (sans-serif) | Body text, descriptions, UI | Both are loaded via `next/font/google` and injected as CSS variables (`--font-fraunces`, `--font-inter`) in the root layout. ### Animation All animation uses Framer Motion. The rules: - **Spring physics only.** No CSS easing, no `tween`. Use `{ type: "spring", stiffness: 200, damping: 20 }`. - **Fade-up reveals.** Initial state: `opacity: 0, y: 30`. Animate to: `opacity: 1, y: 0`. - **Staggered delays.** Use `delay: index * 0.1` for list items. - **Scroll-triggered.** Use `whileInView` with `viewport={{ once: true }}`. - **Hover effects.** Use `whileHover` and `whileTap` on interactive elements. - **No sci-fi effects.** No glows, no particle systems, no 3D transforms. The motion language is playful and springy, not sleek and corporate. - **Respect reduced motion.** The global CSS handles this via `prefers-reduced-motion`. Components should not duplicate this logic. ### Decorative Components Decorative elements follow strict accessibility rules: - Always include `aria-hidden="true"` on decorative elements. - Always include `pointer-events-none` so they don't interfere with interaction. - Use responsive visibility (`hidden sm:block`, `hidden lg:block`) to hide on small screens where they might obscure content. - Decorative components live in `src/components/decorative/` and are exported from the barrel `index.ts`. ### Page Pattern Every page follows this structure: ```tsx // 1. Import metadata type (for static metadata) or generateMetadata function import type { Metadata } from "next"; // 2. Import Contentlayer types import { allProjects } from "contentlayer/generated"; // 3. Import sorting/comparison utilities import { compareDesc } from "date-fns"; // 4. Import client component for interactive parts import { ProjectsPageContent } from "@/components/projects/ProjectsPageContent"; // 5. Static metadata export export const metadata: Metadata = { title: "Projects", description: "...", }; // 6. Default function — server component, data fetching at top export default function ProjectsPage() { const projects = allProjects.sort((a, b) => compareDesc(new Date(a.date), new Date(b.date)), ); return ; } ``` Dynamic routes use `generateStaticParams()` for static generation and `generateMetadata()` for per-page SEO. ### API Route Pattern API routes live in `src/app/api/[name]/route.ts`: - Export named HTTP method handlers: `export async function GET()`, `export async function POST()`. - Return `NextResponse.json()` with explicit `Cache-Control` headers. - Export `revalidate` for ISR caching where applicable. - Use Zod for request body validation. - Always return proper error status codes with JSON error bodies. ### Barrel Exports Each component directory has an `index.ts` barrel file that re-exports all components: ```tsx export { StickerTag } from "./StickerTag"; export { DoodleAccent } from "./DoodleAccent"; export { PaperCard } from "./PaperCard"; // ... ``` Import from the barrel, not individual files: ```tsx import { StickerTag, DoodleAccent } from "@/components/decorative"; ``` ### MDX Content #### Frontmatter All required fields must be present. Dates use ISO format (`YYYY-MM-DD`). Tags are lowercase strings. Status values are one of `active`, `experimental`, or `archived`. #### Images Use standard Markdown image syntax with alt text: ```mdx ![Alt text describing the image](/images/projects/example.png) ``` #### Headings Start at `##` (h2). The page title comes from frontmatter and renders as h1. Use `###` for subsections within h2 sections. ### Dark Mode The site uses the `class` strategy for dark mode (`next-themes`). Dark mode colours are defined as `.dark` variants in Tailwind config and in global CSS. When adding new colours or components: - Use Tailwind's `dark:` prefix for dark-mode overrides. - The `bg-background` / `bg-background-dark` tokens handle page backgrounds. - Accent colours (mustard, teal, salmon, etc.) remain the same in both modes. - Text and background colours invert; borders shift to lighter values. ## Development Process The project was built in phases: 1. **Foundation** — Next.js initialisation, Tailwind configuration with the custom token layer, typography setup (Fraunces + Inter via `next/font`), and layout primitives (Container, Section, Navbar, Footer). 2. **Content layer** — Contentlayer configuration with Project, Article, and Experiment schemas. MDX plugins for syntax highlighting, slug generation, and auto-linked headings. Dynamic route generation with `generateStaticParams()`. 3. **Pages** — Homepage with hero, featured projects grid, writing preview, and GitHub activity sections. Individual project and article pages with editorial layouts. 4. **Design system** — Decorative component library (StickerTag, DoodleAccent, TapeFrame, WobbleCard, PaperCard, CrookedDivider, NoiseOverlay, ScribbleArrow). Noise texture overlay. Paper-grain CSS utility. 5. **Interactivity** — Framer Motion animation system. GitHub API integration with polling. Project filtering by status. Responsive design pass. ## Challenges ### Balancing Chaos and Usability The biggest challenge was maintaining readability while making everything look intentionally imperfect. Crooked cards need to still be clickable. Oversized typography needs to still flow on mobile. Doodles need to not obscure content. Every decorative element uses `pointer-events-none`, `aria-hidden="true"`, and responsive visibility classes (`hidden sm:block`, `hidden lg:block`) to ensure they enhance rather than interfere. ### Static Content with Live Data The site mixes statically-generated MDX content with live GitHub API data. Contentlayer handles the static side at build time, while API routes and client-side polling handle the dynamic side. The GitHub section uses `useEffect` with `setInterval` for polling, `useState` for optimistic updates, and graceful fallbacks when the API is unavailable or rate-limited. ### SVG Favicon The site uses an SVG favicon (`src/app/icon.svg`) — a hand-drawn sandwich in the site's colour palette (mustard bread, green lettuce, salmon filling). Next.js App Router automatically serves this as the favicon without additional configuration. ## Future Improvements - **Search** — Full-text search across projects and articles using Typesense, with fuzzy matching and tag filtering. - **Command palette** — A `cmdk`-powered command palette for keyboard-driven navigation. - **Embedded demos** — Inline iframe embeds of live project demos directly within project pages. - **RSS feed** — An RSS feed for the writing section. - **Analytics** — Privacy-friendly analytics via Plausible. - **OG image generation** — Dynamic OpenGraph images per project using `@vercel/og`. ## Links - [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/ppsandwich/sandwich-dot-codes) - [Live Site](https://sandwich-dot-codes.vercel.app)
